title: REDI-NET F-3 FECES STORAGE
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f1fmj","text":"The overarching aim of the REDI-NET is to develop a collaborative laboratory network between domestic and international partnering institutions to address disease surveillance needs in order to effectively detect, predict and contain potentially emergent zoonosis. This SOP provides guidance on storage of fecal samples and their purified nucleic acid to preserve their integrity for downstream nucleic acid extraction and/or sequencing library preparation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5345o","text":"To outline steps for properly storing feces samples and nucleic acid samples purified from these samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Precool 96-well microfuge tube racks on ice. NOTE: Fecal samples need to be kept on cold chain all the time to prevent RNA degradation. The following procedure will apply only where -80 °C storage is feasible. NOTE: If -80 °C storage is not possible, temporarily store the fecal samples in a -20 °C freezer and follow fecal sample processing SOP (REDI-NET SOP F-2 Feces Processing) as soon as possible for total nucleic acid extraction. Subsequently, use a portion of the total nucleic acid and reverse- transcribe RNA into cDNA for -20 °C storage. To do this, follow the initial steps of the fecal sample testing SOP (REDI-NET SOP F-4 Feces Testing) until finishing the step of “Removal of RNA”.
Using preprinted adhesive labels or permanent markers, label 1.5 mL microfuge tubes with unique sample ID.
Transfer fecal sample into the corresponding pre-labeled 1.5 mL microfuge tubes and put it onto the microfuge tube rack (on ice) sequentially.
Once the rack is full or all fecal samples have been completed, label the rack with a unique rack ID.
Close the rack lid tightly, secure with clear saran wrap and immediately transfer to -80 °C freezer.
Enter the storage location in the REDI-NET Data Portal using the Sample Storage tab. See Guidance on REDI-NET Sample Storage Time QRG.

NOTE: The following procedure is to properly store total nucleic acid extracted from fecal samples (including negative controls) using KingFisher nucleic acid purification system. The eluted total nucleic acid will be in either 96-well microplate (Flex model) or elution strip (Duo Prime model). NOTE: Total nucleic acid samples need to be kept on ice all the time and as short as possible to minimize RNA degradation. In the clean PCR workstation, carefully transfer the eluted total nucleic acid to a 96-well PCR microplate, making sure to keep samples in the exact same locations (e.g., A1 to A1, A2 to A2…) corresponding to the rack where the original fecal samples were stored. IMPORTANT: Mark the “A1” position on the 96-well microplate to avoid any mistakes on plate orientation.
Cover the 96-well PCR microplate with adhesive film to prevent spill over or contamination.
Label the film with a unique plate ID.
Immediately transfer the 96-well PCR microplate to -80 °C freezer.
creator: REDI-NET Consortium
